author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VII. 14–18.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What follows of the Gospel and was read to-day, we must next in order look at, and speak from it as the Lord may grant us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yesterday it was read thus far, that although they had not seen the Lord Jesus in the temple on the feast-day, yet they were speaking about Him: “And some said, He is a good man: but others said, Nay; but he seduceth the people.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this was said for the comfort of those who, afterwards preaching God’s word, were to be seducers, and yet true men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if to seduce is to deceive, neither was Christ a seducer, nor His apostles, nor ought any Christian to be such; but if to seduce (to lead aside) is by persuading to lead one from something to something else, we ought to inquire into the whence and the whither: if from evil to good, the seducer is a good man; if from good to evil, the seducer is a bad man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that sense, then, in which men are seduced from evil to good, would that all of us both were called, and actually were seducers!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then afterwards the Lord went up to the feast, “about the middle of the feast, and taught.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who was in secret taught, He was speaking openly and was not restrained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that hiding of Himself was for the sake of example; this showing Himself openly was an intimation of His power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as He taught, “the Jews marvelled;” all indeed, so far as I think, marvelled, but all were not converted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why this wondering?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because all knew where He was born, where He had been brought up; they had never seen Him learning letters, but they heard Him disputing about the law, bringing forward testimonies of the law, which none could bring forward unless he had read, and none could read unless he had learned letters: and therefore they marvelled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But their marvelling was made an occasion to the Master of insinuating the truth more deeply into their minds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By reason, indeed of their wondering and words, the Lord said something profound, and worthy of being more diligently looked into and discussed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On account of which I would urge you, my beloved, to earnestness, not only in hearing for yourselves, but also in praying for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then did the Lord answer those that were marvelling how He knew letters which He had not learned?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “My doctrine,” saith He, “is not mine, but His that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the first profundity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He seems as if in a few words He had spoken contraries.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He says not, This doctrine is not mine; but, “My doctrine is not mine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If not Thine, how Thine?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If Thine, how not Thine?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Thou sayest both: both, “my doctrines;” and, “not mine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He had said, This doctrine is not mine, there would have been no question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, brethren, in the first place, consider well the question, and so in due order expect the solution.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he who sees not the question proposed, how can he understand what is expounded?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The subject of inquiry, then, is that which He says, “My, not mine” this appears to be contrary; how “my,” how “not mine”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we carefully look at what the holy evangelist himself says in the beginning of his Gospel, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” thence hangs the solution of this question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is the doctrine of the Father, but the Father’s Word?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, Christ Himself is the doctrine of the Father, if He is the Word of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since the Word cannot be of none, but of some one, He said both “His doctrine,” namely, Himself, and also, “not His own,” because He is the Word of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is so much “Thine” as Thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what so much not Thine as Thyself, if that Thou art is of another?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word then is God; and it is also the Word of a stable, unchangeable doctrine, not such as can be sounded by syllables and fleeting, but abiding with the Father, to which abiding doctrine let us be converted, being admonished by the transitory sounds of the voice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that which is transitory does not so admonish us as to call us to transitory things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are admonished to love God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this that I have said were syllables; they smote through the air to reach your sense of hearing, and by sounding passed away: that, however, which I advise you ought not so to pass away, because He whom I exhort you to love passes not away; and when you, exhorted in transient syllables, shall have been converted, you shall not pass away, but shall abide with Him who is abiding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is therefore in the doctrine this great matter, this deep and eternal thing which is permanent: whither all things that pass away in time call us, when they mean well and are not falsely put forward.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, in fact, all the signs which we produce by sounds do signify something which is not sound.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God is not the two short syllables “Deus,” and it is not the two short syllables that we worship, and it is not the two short syllables that we adore, nor is it to the two short syllables that we desire to come—two syllables which almost cease to sound before they have begun to sound; nor in sounding them is there room for the second until the first has passed away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There remains, then, something great which is called “God,” although the sound does not remain when we say the word “God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus direct your thoughts to the doctrine of Christ, and ye shall arrive at the Word of God; and when you have arrived at the Word of God, consider this, “The Word was God,” and you will see that it was said truly, “my doctrine:” consider also whose the Word is, and you will see that it was rightly said, “is not mine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, to speak briefly, beloved, it seems to me that the Lord Jesus Christ said, “My doctrine is not mine,” meaning the same thing as if He said, “I am not from myself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For although we say and believe that the Son is equal to the Father, and that there is not any diversity of nature and substance in them, that there has not intervened any interval of time between Him that begets and Him that is begotten, nevertheless we say these things, while keeping and guarding this, that the one is the Father, the other the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Father He is not if He have not a Son, and Son He is not if He have not a Father: but yet the Son is God from the Father; and the Father is God, but not from the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father of the Son, not God from the Son: but the other is Son of the Father, and God from the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord Christ is called Light from Light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Light then which is not from Light, and the equal Light which is from Light, are together one Light not two Lights.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we have understood this, thanks be to God; but if any has not sufficiently understood, man has done as far as he could: as for the rest, let him see whence he may hope to understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As laborers outside, we can plant and water; but it is of God to give the increase.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “My doctrine,” saith He, “is not mine, but His that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him who says he has not yet understood hear counsel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For since it was a great and profound matter that had been spoken, the Lord Christ Himself did certainly see that all would not understand this so profound a matter, and He gave counsel in the sequel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to understand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For God has said by the prophet: “Except ye believe, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the same purpose what the Lord here also added as He went on—“If any man is willing to do His will, he shall know concerning the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak from myself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the meaning of this, “If any man be willing to do His will”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I had said, if any man believe; and I gave this counsel: If thou hast not understood, said I, believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For understanding is the reward of faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore do not seek to understand in order to believe, but believe that thou mayest understand; since, “except ye believe, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when I would counsel the obedience of believing toward the possibility of understanding, and say that our Lord Jesus Christ has added this very thing in the following sentence, we find Him to have said, “If any man be willing to do His will, he shall know of the doctrine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is “he shall know”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the same thing as “he shall understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is “If any man be willing to do His will”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the same thing as to believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All men indeed perceive that “shall know” is the same thing as “shall understand:” but that the saying, “If any man be willing to do His will,” refers to believing, all do not perceive; to perceive this more accurately, we need the Lord Himself for expounder, to show us whether the doing of the Father’s will does in reality refer to believing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who does not know that this is to do the will of God, to work the work of God; that is, to work that work which is pleasing to Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord Himself says openly in another place: “This is the work of God, that ye believe on Him whom He has sent.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That ye believe on Him,” not, that ye believe Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if ye believe on Him, ye believe Him; yet he that believes Him does not necessarily believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even the devils believed Him, but they did not believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, moreover, of His apostles we can say, we believe Paul; but not, we believe on Paul: we believe Peter; but not, we believe on Peter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, “to him that believeth on Him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted unto him for righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then is “to believe on Him”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By believing to love Him, by believing to esteem highly, by believing to go into Him and to be incorporated in His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is faith itself then that God exacts from us: and He finds not that which He exacts, unless He has bestowed what He may find.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What faith, but that which the apostle has most amply defined in another place, saying, “Neither circumcision availeth anything, nor uncircumcision, but faith that worketh by love?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not any faith of what kind soever, but “faith that worketh by love:” let this faith be in thee, and thou shall understand concerning the doctrine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What indeed shall thou understand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That “this doctrine is not mine, but His that sent me;” that is, thou shall understand that Christ the Son of God, who is the doctrine of the Father, is not from Himself, but is the Son of the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This sentence overthrows the Sabellian heresy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Sabellians have dared to affirm that the Son is the very same as He who is also the Father: that the names are two, but the reality one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the names were two and reality one, it would not be said, “My doctrine is not mine.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Anyhow, if Thy doctrine is not Thine, O Lord, whose is it, unless there be another whose it is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Sabellians understand not what Thou saidst; for they see not the trinity, but follow the error of their own heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us worshippers of the trinity and unity of Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, and one God, understand concerning Christ’s doctrine, how it is not His.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He said that He spoke not from Himself for this reason, because Christ is the Son of the Father, and the Father is the Father of Christ; and the Son is from God the Father, God, but God the Father is God not from God the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: ‘This will be he who is called Antichrist,’ exalting himself,” as the apostle says, “above all that is called God, and that is worshipped.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, declaring that this same it is that will seek his own glory, not the glory of the Father, says to the Jews: “I am come in my Father’s name, and ye have not received me; another will come in his own name, him ye will receive.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He intimated that they would receive Antichrist, who will seek the glory of his own name, puffed up, not solid; and therefore not stable, but assuredly ruinous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But our Lord Jesus Christ has shown us a great example of humility: for doubtless He is equal with the Father, for “in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God;” yea, doubtless, He Himself said, and most truly said, “Am I so long time with you, and ye have not known me, Philip?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that hath seen me hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, doubtless, Himself said, and most truly said, “I and the Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, He is one with the Father, equal to the Father, God from God, God with God, coeternal, immortal, alike unchangeable, alike without time, alike Creator and disposer of times; and yet because He came in time, and took the form of a servant, and in condition was found as a man, He seeks the glory of the Father, not His own; what oughtest thou to do, O man, who, when thou doest anything good, seekest thy own glory; but when thou doest anything ill, dost meditate calumny against God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider thyself: thou art a creature, acknowledge thy Creator: thou art a servant, despise not thy Lord: thou art adopted, not for thy own merits; seek His glory from whom thou hast this grace, that thou art a man adopted; His, whose glory He sought who is from Him, the Only-begotten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But He that seeketh His glory that sent Him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In Antichrist, however, there is unrighteousness, and he is not true; because he will seek his own glory, not His by whom he was sent: for, indeed, he was not sent, but only permitted to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us all, therefore, that belong to the body of Christ, seek not our own glory, that we be not led into the snares of Antichrist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if Christ sought His glory that sent Him, how much more ought we to seek the glory of Him who made us?
